com.mentor.chs.api

Interface IXWire

  • All Superinterfaces:
    IXAbstractConductor, IXActiveObject, IXConnectivityObject, IXHighwayConductor, IXLibrariedObject, IXModuledObject, IXObject, IXWriteableObject
    All Known Subinterfaces:
    IXVariantWire

    public interface IXWire
extends IXHighwayConductor, IXModuledObject, IXActiveObject
    Interface providing access to the center-strip splices, signal, harness and harness levels of this wire.

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Instance Methods Abstract Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      java.util.Set<? extends IXCenterStripConnection> getCenterstripConnections()
      Retrieves the set of center strips on this wire.
      java.util.Set<IXSplice> getCenterstripSplices()
      Get the center-strip splices associated with this wire.
      java.util.Set<? extends IXConductorVariant> getConductorVariants()
      Retrieve information about the variants of a conductor when the signal map of its start and/or end connector associates the conductor with multiple cavities.
      IXHarness getHarness()
      Get the harness containing this wire.
      java.util.Set<IXHarnessLevel> getHarnessLevels()
      Get the harness levels on which this wire is included.
      java.util.List<? extends IXNode> getLoopbackNodes()
      Get the loopback nodes on the routing path of this wire.
      java.util.List<IXNode> getNodes()
      Get the nodes defining the routing path of this wire.
      java.util.List<IXNode> getNotThroughNodes()
      Get the List of Not-through nodes set on this wire.
      IXSignal getSignal()
      Get the signal implemented by this wire.
      java.util.List<IXNode> getThroughNodes()
      Get the list of Through nodes set on this wire.
      java.util.Set<IXWireEnd> getWireEnds()
      Get the wire ends associated with this wire.
      java.util.Set<IXMarker> getWireMarkers()
      Get wire markers associated with this wire.
      boolean isPokeHomePin(IXAbstractPin pin)
      Find if the wire is poked at the pin.

    • Method Detail

      • getCenterstripSplices

        java.util.Set<IXSplice> getCenterstripSplices()
        Get the center-strip splices associated with this wire. Note that wires and shields terminated at the pins of this splice are accessed via the getPins() method.
        Returns:
        a Set of center-strip splices associated with this wire, an empty Set otherwise.

      • getSignal

        IXSignal getSignal()
        Get the signal implemented by this wire. Note that this method only has relevance if the wire exists in a Capital Integrator design.
        Returns:
        the signal implemented by this wire if it exists, null otherwise.

      • getHarness

        IXHarness getHarness()
        Get the harness containing this wire. Note that this method only has relevance if the wire exists in a Capital Integrator design.
        Returns:
        the harness containing this wire if it exists, null otherwise.

      • getHarnessLevels

        java.util.Set<IXHarnessLevel> getHarnessLevels()
        Get the harness levels on which this wire is included. Note that this method only has relevance if the wire exists in a Capital Integrator design.
        Returns:
        a set of harness levels on which this wire exists if any, an empty Set otherwise.

      • getWireEnds

        java.util.Set<IXWireEnd> getWireEnds()
        Get the wire ends associated with this wire.
        Returns:
        Set<IXWireEnd> Wire ends associated with this wire.

      • getWireMarkers

        java.util.Set<IXMarker> getWireMarkers()
        Get wire markers associated with this wire.
        Returns:
        Set<IXMarker> Wire markers associated with this wire.

      • getNodes

        java.util.List<IXNode> getNodes()
        Get the nodes defining the routing path of this wire.
        Returns:
        List<IXNode> Routing path of this wire.
        See Also:
        getLoopbackNodes()

      • getLoopbackNodes

        java.util.List<? extends IXNode> getLoopbackNodes()
        Get the loopback nodes on the routing path of this wire.
        A loopback node is one at which a wire approaches and leaves the node in the same direction (forming a loop in the wire).
        The nodes will be ordered according to their position in the path of the wire.
        Returns:
        The loopback nodes on the routing path of this wire.

      • getThroughNodes

        java.util.List<IXNode> getThroughNodes()
        Get the list of Through nodes set on this wire.
        Returns:
        List<IXNode> if through nodes exists for this wire, EmptyList otherwise

      • getNotThroughNodes

        java.util.List<IXNode> getNotThroughNodes()
        Get the List of Not-through nodes set on this wire.
        Returns:
        List<IXNode> if not-through nodes exists for this wire, EmptyList otherwise

      • getConductorVariants

        java.util.Set<? extends IXConductorVariant> getConductorVariants()
        Retrieve information about the variants of a conductor when the signal map of its start and/or end connector associates the conductor with multiple cavities. Note that this method only has relevance if the conductor exists in a Capital Integrator design.
        Returns:
        The variances (the empty set if there are no variances).

      • isPokeHomePin

        boolean isPokeHomePin(IXAbstractPin pin)
        Find if the wire is poked at the pin. The Poked status on this pin is true in two cases, as below 1. If user has marked the wire as poke home at the end of pin, in poke home tab of wire's properties dialog. 2. If the harness attribute of the PinList of the pin is different from the harness of the wire. This condition is evaluated only when the preference for this is set to true on project at Preferences -> CapitalLogic -> Wire.
        Parameters:
        pin - is the value upon which the wire is tested for poke home.
        Returns:
        true if the wire is poked at the pin otherwise false

      • getCenterstripConnections

        java.util.Set<? extends IXCenterStripConnection> getCenterstripConnections()
        Retrieves the set of center strips on this wire. Each of these objects should have an associated splice which is also in the returned center strip splices set. The returned set is not ordered, please use the Order attribute on the returned objects if needed.
        Returns:
        The set of center strips on this wire.
